verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a type I single-pass TM protein primarily resident in the ER, but translocates to the plasma membrane upon ER Ca²⁺ store depletion and during mitosis. The protein reaches the outer PM face via its own TM domain and forms a stable complex with Orai1 at the PM to activate store-operated Ca²⁺ entry. This conditional, stimulus-induced surface display qualifies as dual_localization.
